description: Industry-aware Q2 and Q4 questions with skill mapping. Each industry gets tailored motivation tiles (Q2) and story patterns (Q4) that extract more precise skills than generic questions. The industry context also filters job postings and weights skills differently.

q2.heading: What makes a great shift for you?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about something you're proud of from work
q4.subtitle: A shift, a moment with a customer, something you handled well — big or small.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I handled a fully booked Friday service on my own, I turned a complaint into a 5-star review, I trained 3 new starters who all stayed...
q4.ai_context: The user works in hospitality or food service (restaurant, cafe, hotel, or travel). Interpret their story through this lens — 'managing a section' means operational coordination, 'dealing with a difficult table' means conflict resolution and composure, 'upselling' means persuasion and product knowledge. Extract transferable professional skills that would resonate with employers outside hospitality.

story_patterns.pattern: handled a difficult or angry customer
ran a busy service or shift alone
trained new staff
upsold or recommended products
managed a complaint that turned into a positive
organised or reorganised a process

q2.heading: What makes a great day on the floor?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about something you handled really well
q4.subtitle: A customer situation, a tough shift, something you solved — big or small.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I de-escalated an angry customer and kept them as a regular, I reorganised the stockroom and cut pick time in half, I hit 130% of my sales target during the quietest month of the year...
q4.ai_context: The user works in retail or customer service. 'Handling a difficult customer' signals conflict resolution and empathy. 'Hitting targets' signals sales ability and resilience. 'Organising the shop floor' signals operational thinking. Extract transferable professional skills that show their value beyond a retail context.

story_patterns.pattern: dealt with a difficult customer or complaint
hit or exceeded a sales target
organised or improved a store process
trained or mentored a colleague
solved a problem no one else could fix

q2.heading: What makes a great day on the ward or in clinic?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about something you're proud of clinically or professionally
q4.subtitle: A patient outcome, a difficult situation, something you did that made a difference.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I caught a medication error before it reached a patient, I supported a family through a really difficult diagnosis, I redesigned a handover process that reduced errors on our ward...
q4.ai_context: The user works in healthcare or medicine. Clinical stories contain rich transferable skills — 'catching a medication error' means attention to detail and clinical governance, 'supporting a patient through a difficult diagnosis' means empathy and emotional intelligence, 'coordinating a complex discharge' means project management and multi-agency working. Extract the professional and transferable skills behind the clinical story.

story_patterns.pattern: caught an error or prevented harm
supported a patient or family through something difficult
diagnosed or identified something others missed
improved a ward or clinic process
coordinated care across multiple teams
trained or educated a colleague or patient

q2.heading: What makes a great day at your desk?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about something you built or solved that you're proud of
q4.subtitle: A project, a bug fix, a system — technical or otherwise.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I built a feature solo that shipped on time, I reduced deployment time by 40%, I diagnosed a production issue at 2am and fixed it before users noticed, I migrated a legacy system to cloud with zero downtime...
q4.ai_context: The user works in technology or engineering. Extract both technical and soft skills from their story. 'Fixed a production bug' signals debugging, systems thinking, and resilience under pressure. 'Migrated to cloud' signals cloud architecture, project management, and risk management. 'Explained to stakeholders' signals technical communication. Look for the professional skills that make this person valuable beyond just their technical stack.

story_patterns.pattern: built or shipped a feature or product
fixed a critical or difficult bug
improved performance or reduced costs
led or coordinated a technical project
migrated or modernised a system
mentored or helped a colleague technically

q2.heading: What does a great day at work feel like for you?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about something you delivered or improved at work
q4.subtitle: A project, a process, a result — anything you feel good about.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I implemented a new reporting system that saved 5 hours a week, I coordinated a team through a difficult product launch, I reduced supplier costs by 15% through renegotiation...
q4.ai_context: The user works in business, operations, HR, procurement, or administration. Their stories contain rich operational and strategic skills. 'Coordinating a project launch' signals project management and stakeholder communication. 'Renegotiating a contract' signals negotiation and commercial acumen. 'Fixing a broken process' signals analytical thinking and initiative. Extract the professional skills at the core of each story.

story_patterns.pattern: delivered a project on time or under budget
improved or redesigned a process
negotiated a better deal or outcome
led or managed people through change
turned data into a decision or recommendation
hired or developed a team member

q2.heading: What makes a great day in your role?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about a result you're proud of
q4.subtitle: A deal, a campaign, a target — anything that shows what you're capable of.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I closed our biggest deal of the quarter after 3 months of nurturing, I ran a campaign that doubled email open rates, I hit 120% of my annual target during a market downturn...
q4.ai_context: The user works in sales or marketing. 'Closing a deal' signals negotiation, persuasion, and resilience. 'A campaign that performed' signals creativity, analytics, and execution. 'Growing an account' signals relationship management and strategic thinking. Extract the professional skills that transfer beyond their specific sales or marketing context.

story_patterns.pattern: closed a deal or won new business
ran a campaign that exceeded targets
grew or retained an account
built a pipeline or audience from scratch
used data to improve performance

q2.heading: What makes a great creative day for you?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about something you created that you're proud of
q4.subtitle: A project, a piece of work, a creative challenge you solved.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I designed a brand identity that got picked up in a trade magazine, I edited a video that went viral, I wrote a campaign that converted 3x better than the previous one, I delivered a full shoot in conditions that nearly made it impossible...
q4.ai_context: The user works in a creative or media field. Creative stories contain strong transferable skills — 'delivering under a tight deadline' means time management and resilience, 'managing a difficult client' means communication and stakeholder management, 'redesigning something that underperformed' means analytical thinking and creative problem solving. Extract both the creative and the professional skills embedded in the story.

story_patterns.pattern: created something that got recognition or performed well
delivered under a tight deadline or difficult conditions
managed a difficult client or stakeholder
solved a brief that seemed impossible
translated a vague brief into a clear output

q2.heading: What makes a great day in finance feel like?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about something you spotted or delivered that made a difference
q4.subtitle: A financial insight, a project, a process you improved.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I identified a tax saving that saved the company £40k, I completed year-end two weeks early, I built a financial model that changed a major investment decision, I caught a fraud risk before it was exploited...
q4.ai_context: The user works in finance or accounting. Extract both technical and soft skills — 'identifying a tax saving' signals analytical thinking, financial acumen, and initiative. 'Completing year-end early' signals time management, accuracy, and resilience under pressure. 'Explaining a complex financial position to a board' signals financial communication and stakeholder management. Look beyond the numbers to the professional skills.

story_patterns.pattern: found a financial saving or identified a risk
completed a complex process under time pressure
built a model or report that influenced a decision
caught a compliance or fraud issue
negotiated or closed a financial deal

q2.heading: What makes a great day with the people you support?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about something you did that made a real difference
q4.subtitle: A student, a client, a moment — big or small.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I helped a struggling student pass their exams after months of extra support, I advocated for a client and got them housing they'd been denied for years, I designed a group session that finally worked for a class that had been disengaged all year...
q4.ai_context: The user works in education or social care. Their stories contain deep transferable skills — 'helping a struggling student' signals patience, differentiated communication, and resilience. 'Advocating for a client' signals negotiation, empathy, and persistence. 'Managing a complex safeguarding case' signals risk assessment, multi-agency working, and documentation. Extract the professional transferable skills that would resonate with employers beyond education and social care.

story_patterns.pattern: helped someone achieve something they were struggling with
advocated for someone and got results
handled a safeguarding or sensitive situation
designed or delivered a programme or session
worked across teams or agencies to get an outcome

q2.heading: What makes a great day on site or on the job?
q2.subtitle: Pick the one that sounds most like you.

q4.heading: Tell us about something on the job you're proud of
q4.subtitle: A difficult job, a problem you solved, something you built or fixed.
q4.placeholder: e.g. I diagnosed an electrical fault that three other electricians had missed, I delivered a full kitchen fit-out two days early, I coordinated 6 different trades on a complex renovation without a single delay...
q4.ai_context: The user works in trades or construction. Their stories contain rich professional skills often undervalued — 'diagnosing a fault others missed' signals analytical thinking, technical depth, and persistence. 'Coordinating multiple contractors' signals project management and communication. 'Delivering a job early' signals planning and client management. Extract transferable professional skills that translate beyond the specific trade.

story_patterns.pattern: diagnosed a fault or problem others couldn't solve
completed a complex job to a high standard
coordinated or managed a site or team
communicated with a client and managed their expectations
found a smarter or faster way to do something
trained or mentored someone

scoring_impact.description: How industry selection changes the matching engine

changes.detail: Results are pre-filtered to postings in the industry's posting_categories before scoring. A Healthcare user won't see Technology postings unless their skills also match. This prevents irrelevant matches.
Student path users also filter by seniority_filter from their industry. Healthcare students won't see Senior clinical roles.

The Q3 Claude API call includes the ai_context string, so identical stories from different industries extract different skills. 'I managed a difficult situation' means conflict resolution for hospitality but clinical governance for healthcare.

Industry-specific Q2 tiles have different amplifies_q1_tiles arrays than the generic version. The overlap check is now more precise — a hospitality user who selects 'I kept calm when everything got chaotic' amplifies different Q1 tiles than a finance user who selects 'I completed something complex accurately under pressure'.
Each Q2 tile now includes suggested_titles and boost_posting_categories specific to the industry context. These get the +0.1 score bonus on top of the base match score.
